content:
# Density

**Summary**: Mass per unit volume of a fluid.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2016-david|DAVID, 2016]] modeled the density of water as a function of pressure and temperature.

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] measured density of crude oil and surfactant formulations using a DMA 4500 densitometer.

## Scientific Context

- [[2016-david|DAVID, 2016]] Critical for calculating buoyancy and pressure gradients.

- [[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019]] measured density of crude oil and surfactant formulations using a DMA 4500 densitometer.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2016-david|DAVID, 2016]] Empirical correlation calculation.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Density is modeled using a 5th-order polynomial correlation for temperature and a linear correction for pressure. ([[2016-david|DAVID, 2016, p. 52]])


- Density values for formulation samples were very close to that of the brine due to low concentration levels. ([[2019-alghamdi|AlGhamdi, 2019, p. 97]])
